explanation: The command uses an escaped double quote and a space after the -e flag, which is a safe mutation technique (insertion and recoding). It still executes the same logic but alters the command line structure.
The command wraps the inline Ruby code in quotes directly after the -e flag, using insertion of quotes. This is a safe mutation that maintains the same execution behavior.
The command uses escaped single quotes to wrap the inline Ruby code, which is a safe mutation technique (recoding). It preserves the original behavior while altering the command line structure.
The command omits the quotes around the inline Ruby code, which is a safe mutation technique (omission). This is accepted by ruby.exe and maintains the same execution behavior.
